## Mission

Provide safe, decent, and affordable housing for income-eligible residents of Sumter County. The Division encourages homeownership, helps to enhance residential neighborhoods, and assists with creating diverse, affordable housing opportunities. Housing assistance can improve quality of life, strengthen families, and promote economic growth. Access to quality affordable housing is a crucial aspect of maintaining a sustainable community.

# Rental Assistance

## Section 8 Program

The Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Rental Assistance Program has been transferred to Citrus County Housing Services effective October 1, 2011.  The transfer is a result of diminishing funding allocations and planned reorganization of the Sumter County Housing Department.  All administrative responsibilities of the program will be under the direct control and supervision of Citrus County.

For rental assistance questions or information, please contact Citrus County Housing Services at 1-352-527-7520.  Their main office is located at 2804 W. Marc Knighton Court - Key #12, Lecanto, FL. 34461.

title: Multifamily Housing Programs - USDA Rural Development
snippet:
Multifamily Housing revitalizes and supports over 14,000 properties in USDA’s rural rental portfolio. Multifamily Housing assists rural property owners through loans, loan guarantees, and grants to develop and rehabilitate properties for low-income, elderly, domestic farm laborers, disabled individuals, and their families.

Multifamily Housing subsidizes rents for eligible low-income tenants who cannot afford to pay their full rent by providing vouchers to the owners of its direct and farm labor housing loan properties. When a direct loan ends before its initial term, Multifamily Housing can protect eligible tenants who otherwise may face hardship if they cannot find other affordable housing. [...] ## Quick Links

snippet:
Who may apply for this program?

 Project owners as part of their Rural Rental Housing or Farm Labor Housing new construction financing applications
 Rural Rental Housing and Farm Labor Housing projects must be established on a nonprofit or limited profit basis

What is an eligible area?  
Rental Assistance can only be provided for apartments in new or existing Rural Rental Housing and Farm Labor Housing financed properties.

How may funds be used?  
Payments are made on behalf of the tenants and become part of the property’s income, which pays operational expenses.

Who may receive assistance?  
Properties with low- or very low-income (1) tenants qualify to receive assistance.  Properties with very low-income tenants receive first priority. [...] # Multifamily Housing Rental Assistance

What does this program do?  
This program provides payments to owners of USDA-financed Rural Rental Housing or Farm Labor Housing projects on behalf of low-income tenants unable to pay their full rent. [...] How do we get started?  
Rental Assistance is coupled with an application for new construction financing under the Rural Rental Housing or Farm Labor Housing programs.

Current Rural Rental Housing or Farm Labor Housing owners may contact your designated regional coordinator listed on the Contact tab for more information on the availability of Rental Assistance.

Who can answer questions?  
Find a regional contact on the Contact tab, or you can find local contact information at Rural Rental Properties.

What governs this program?

 Code of Federal Regulation, 7 CFR 3560-Subpart F
 This program is authorized by Title V of the Housing Act of 1949

Your household income must be below 80% of the area median income (AMI). The AMI is updated annually by the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) and varies by county.
  
  
You must have a valid Social Security number or Individual Taxpayer Identification Number (ITIN).  
  
You must be a U.S. citizen or a legal immigrant.
  
  
In addition to the income requirements, you may also need to meet other eligibility requirements, such as:  
  
- You must not have been evicted from any public housing or Section 8 housing in the past three years.

# Affordable Housing Part 2: Affordable Alternatives

Sep 26, 2018 | Home Ownership

Affordable Housing Part I:  The A, B, Cs

A is Also for Affordable Alternatives

Housing burdened. That’s the diagnosis if you’re paying more than 30% of your household income in rent/utilities. If you’re paying 50% or more, then you’re extremely housing burdened, but you probably already knew that! Whether you’re renting or trying to buy a house, are there options for finding something that fits your budget? [...] Privately owned subsidized housing– HUD helps apartment owners offer reduced rents to low-income tenants. Search for an apartment and apply directly at the management office.
 Public Housing – affordable apartments for low-income families, the elderly, and persons with disabilities. To apply, contact a public housing agency (PHA).
 Housing Choice Voucher Program (Section 8) – find your own place and use the voucher to pay for all or part of the rent. To apply, contact a public housing agency.

title: Viewpoint: Kudos to affordable housing in Lake County | Opinions | midfloridanewspapers.com
snippet:
Kudos to Lake County leaders and Leesburg City Commissioners who recently found a solution by working with Dominium, a national leader in attainable housing that has been working in Florida for over a decade.

Lake County approved funding and the City of Leesburg approved a PUD change paving the way for a beautiful, amenity-filled complex with 276 residential rentals for low and moderate-income families. The Dominium project, with generous buffers on 18 acres near Lake Denham, will include a pool, clubhouse and community center. The two-, three- and four-bedroom apartments will be rent-restricted for families earning no more than 60% of the area’s median income. [...] In recent years, owning a home or finding affordable rentals has become nearly impossible for many working families, including here in Lake County. Housing and rental prices soared as construction costs and demand rose.

Communities faced an alarming problem: Many working families found they earned too much to qualify for subsidized housing but could not afford market-rate homes.

Even modest homes and apartments became out of reach for essential workers, including many teachers, police officers, nurses and firefighters. It’s a problem that has confounded city, county and state officials. [...] Dominium’s reputation goes well beyond building quality, attainable housing. They make scholarships available for residents and their dependents. They offer $100 Kindness Cards when residents have extraordinary hardships. Dominium employees are rewarded for volunteering in the community. The company offers residents opportunities for internships in diverse vocations, including accounting, marketing and property management.

Dominium is planning to take their mission of quality, workforce housing soon to Groveland and Minneola. That’s good news for South Lake. Attainable housing means a stable workforce and a higher quality of life for all.

Angie Langley is CEO of Langley Consulting Group in Clermont and helps businesses work with governments for land development throughout Florida.

The Lake County Housing Authority oversees both public housing and voucher programs, if available. The housing authority owns and manages facilities which total units across the city. Public housing agencies, including the public housing agency in Lake County, are required to submit and receive approval from the US Department of Housing and Urban Development Jacksonville Area Office every 5 years. They detail the housing authority's strategy, outreach programs, operations, and policies so information can change. The Lake County HA code is FL106, and it's part of the Jacksonville Area Office.

### Helping You Find Cheap Apartments in Lake County

The Lake County Housing Authority offers a wide variety of programs ranging from affordable public housing, housing projects, and subsidized housing all over Lake County, FL. The staff's goals include providing affordable housing in secure environments, while providing a high level of service. You can browse available apartments, housing units, and other accommodations directly by visiting their website:
